Barring a major policy shift, which could happen as a result of a change in administration, it’s highly unlikely that the U.S. will be able/willing to divorce such groups from the Iraqi government and security forces and maintain the significant presence of U.S.-Coalition forces in country. 4/4 - John M. If the Lebanese government continues to shelter Hezbollah, “fire will come and burn the cedars of Lebanon,” Katz warned. He demanded that Hezbollah be disarmed first south of the Litani River up to the yellow line, and then across all of Lebanon. He added that the IDF is ready to act and assist in the mission. @wfwitness

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 Food for thought 📝 How many more attacks will it take for real action? Events in Mali have significantly dampened celebrations of the annual "Week of Brotherhood," which the countries of the "Sahel trio" — Burkina Faso , Mali , and Niger — had recently positioned as a symbol of the bloc's development and prosperity. Perhaps Mali's destabilization will finally become the event that motivates Sahel governments to engage not only in purely propaganda-oriented activities, but also in measures that have at least some bearing on building up defense capabilities . Burkina Faso has already grasped the scale of the militants' offensive, but responded in its own manner — their recent plans to merge militia with the army were expanded yesterday to mass mobilization. Niger, meanwhile, has not launched any emergency defense measures at all. ❗️ Yet the task of defeating militants is not solved by simply recruiting new military personnel, but by qualitative changes in the armed forces structures themselves. The bloody events of last weekend showed that local armed forces still lack adequate elite units. #BurkinaFaso #Mali #Niger #Sahel 💀 @rybar_africa — where politics runs hotter than the equator 💸 Support us Original msg

The attack in Al-Sawana targeted a disabled Hezbullah fighter, from an old injury that predates this war. Even from the Syrian war. He was killed alongside his wife. Their 3 children and their grandpa were all seriously injured.

🇺🇦 The Director of the Institute of Demography and Social Research of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine Ella Libanova stated that no more than 30% of those who left will return to Ukraine after the war. In 2022, she predicted that number to be 50% but now, taking into account objective processes, she has lowered the forecast to 30%. According to her, people have settled abroad over the four years of war: they have found jobs, their children attend school and kindergarten, they have mastered the language and established connections. At the same time, in Ukraine something is being destroyed every day, people are losing their homes and jobs, even if they had them before. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

text: ELINT News RT @manniefabian : IDF troops captured a cache of Hezbollah weapons in southern Lebanon, including first-person view (FPV) drones. The military says the weapons included anti-tank explosive devices and remote detonation systems, explosive-laden drones, RPGs, firearms, and other weapons. Hezbollah has made frequent use of small FPV drones in its attacks on Israeli troops in recent weeks. tweet
text_zh: ELINT News 转推 @manniefabian：以色列国防军部队在黎巴嫩南部缴获了一批真主党武器，其中包括第一人称视角（FPV）无人机。军方称，这些武器包括反坦克爆炸装置、远程引爆系统、载有爆炸物的无人机、火箭筒、枪支及其他武器。近几周，真主党在针对以色列军队的袭击中频繁使用小型FPV无人机。推文

text: WarMonitor The Middle East conflict is disrupting supplies of key raw materials and driving up prices for printed circuit boards, a core component in everything from phones and computers to AI servers. https://twitter.com/TheWarMonitor/status/2048771649533571447/photo/1 tweet
text_zh: WarMonitor 中东冲突正在扰乱关键原材料的供应，并推高了印刷电路板的价格，这是从手机、电脑到人工智能服务器等所有设备的核心组件。https://twitter.com/TheWarMonitor/status/2048771649533571447/photo/1 推文

text: Open Source Intel Anwar Gargash, diplomatic adviser to the UAE's President: There has always been a debate in the Gulf between the hawks and the doves. The hawks see Iran as a source of danger, and the doves believe that containing Iran is necessary because it is a neighbor. I think this debate has improved today in favor of the hawks; let's be clear about that. In 2000, we signed the Joint Defense Agreement, and we ultimately found that the GCC countries' position was correct logistically, the GCC countries supported each other, but politically and militarily, I think it was the weakest position in history. I expected such a weak stance from the Arab League, and I'm not surprised by it, but I don't expect it from the GCC, and I am surprised by it. tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 阿联酋总统外交顾问安瓦尔·加尔加什：海湾地区在鹰派和鸽派之间一直存在争论。鹰派将伊朗视为危险源，而鸽派则认为遏制伊朗是必要的，因为它是邻国。我认为今天的辩论已经向有利于鹰派的方向发展；这一点必须明确。2000年，我们签署了《共同防御协议》，最终我们发现海湾合作委员会（GCC）国家的立场在后勤上是正确的，GCC国家相互支持，但在政治和军事上，我认为这是历史上最软弱的立场。我预料到阿拉伯国家联盟会采取这种软弱的立场，对此我并不感到惊讶，但我没想到GCC会这样，我对此感到惊讶。推文

text: OSINTdefender According to CNN, citing two sources with knowledge on the matter, U.S. President Donald J. Trump is set to meet with his National Security Team today to discuss next steps in how to move forward with the situation in the Middle East. One topic that will reportedly be discussed is whether or not the U.S. will resume combat operations against Iran. tweet
text_zh: OSINTdefender 据美国有线电视新闻网（CNN）援引两名知情人士的话称，美国总统唐纳德·J·特朗普定于今天会见其国家安全团队，讨论推进中东局势的后续步骤。据报道，将讨论的一个议题是美国是否会恢复针对伊朗的作战行动。推文

text: Open Source Intel Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem in a written statement: We categorically reject direct negotiations with Israel. The Lebanese government must halt direct negotiations, and rescind its decision criminalizing our military wing. We do not recognize these direct negotiations or their outcomes. We will continue our resistance and will not return to the pre–March 2 situation. We will not abandon our weapons and the Israeli enemy will not remain on a single inch of our occupied land, and our people will return to their lands to the very last inch. tweet
text_zh: 开源情报 真主党领导人纳伊姆·卡西姆在书面声明中表示：我们断然拒绝与以色列进行直接谈判。黎巴嫩政府必须停止直接谈判，并撤销其将我们军事派别定为犯罪的决定。我们不承认这些直接谈判及其结果。我们将继续抵抗，不会回到3月2日之前的状态。我们不会放弃武器，以色列敌人不会留在我们被占领土的一寸土地上，我们的人民将回到他们的土地，直到最后一英寸。推文
